queries.query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

arxiv.title: A Novel Approach for Defect Detection of Wind Turbine Blade Using Virtual Reality and Deep Learning
arxiv.abstract: Wind turbines are subjected to continuous rotational stresses and unusual external forces such as storms, lightning, strikes by flying objects, etc., which may cause defects in turbine blades. Hence, it requires a periodical inspection to ensure proper functionality and avoid catastrophic failure. The task of inspection is challenging due to the remote location and inconvenient reachability by human inspection. Researchers used images with cropped defects from the wind turbine in the literature. They neglected possible background biases, which may hinder real-time and autonomous defect detection using aerial vehicles such as drones or others. To overcome such challenges, in this paper, we experiment with defect detection accuracy by having the defects with the background using a two-step deep-learning methodology. In the first step, we develop virtual models of wind turbines to synthesize the near-reality images for four types of common defects - cracks, leading edge erosion, bending, and light striking damage. The Unity perception package is used to generate wind turbine blade defects images with variations in background, randomness, camera angle, and light effects. In the second step, a customized U-Net architecture is trained to classify and segment the defect in turbine blades. The outcomes of U-Net architecture have been thoroughly tested and compared with 5-fold validation datasets. The proposed methodology provides reasonable defect detection accuracy, making it suitable for autonomous and remote inspection through aerial vehicles.
arxiv.source_query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

arxiv.title: An Examination of Rotational Effects on Large Wind Turbine Blades
arxiv.abstract: CFD studies have been performed to demonstrate the effects of rotation on large wind turbine blades. The 10 MW blade of the Advanced Aerodynamic Tools for Large Rotors (AVATAR) project was selected in the present investigation. In order to demonstrate the rotational augmentation, lift coefficients of the blade sections were compared with the two-dimensional simulations at consistent inflow conditions. The increase of the lift coefficient was observed at the inboard section of the blade, showing the effect of rotation. A significant delay of separation point as well as a massive reduction of the wake size occur at the inboard blade section. The results of the present manuscript provide more information of the rotational augmentation occurring in the near hub region of wind turbine blades.
arxiv.source_query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

arxiv.title: Modeling the effect of wind speed and direction shear on utility-scale wind turbine power production
arxiv.abstract: Wind speed and direction variations across the rotor affect power production. As utility-scale turbines extend higher into the atmospheric boundary layer (ABL) with larger rotor diameters and hub heights, they increasingly encounter more complex wind speed and direction variations. We assess three models for power production that account for wind speed and direction shear. Two are based on actuator disc representations and the third is a blade element representation. We also evaluate the predictions from a standard power curve model that has no knowledge of wind shear. The predictions from each model, driven by wind profile measurements from a profiling LiDAR, are compared to concurrent power measurements from an adjacent utility-scale wind turbine. In the field measurements of the utility-scale turbine, discrete combinations of speed and direction shear induce changes in power production of -19% to +34% relative to the turbine power curve for a given hub height wind speed. Positive speed shear generally corresponds to over-performance and positive direction shear to under-performance, relative to the power curve. Overall, the blade element model produces both higher correlation and lower error relative to the other models, but its quantitative accuracy depends on induction and controller sub-models. To further assess the influence of complex, non-monotonic wind profiles, we also drive the models with best-fit power law wind speed profiles and linear wind direction profiles. These idealized inputs produce qualitative and quantitative differences in power predictions from each model, demonstrating that time-varying, non-monotonic wind shear affects wind power production.
arxiv.source_query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

arxiv.title: Wind Turbine Blade Surface Damage Detection based on Aerial Imagery and VGG16-RCNN Framework
arxiv.abstract: In this manuscript, an image analytics based deep learning framework for wind turbine blade surface damage detection is proposed. Turbine blade(s) which carry approximately one-third of a turbine weight are susceptible to damage and can cause sudden malfunction of a grid-connected wind energy conversion system. The surface damage detection of wind turbine blade requires a large dataset so as to detect a type of damage at an early stage. Turbine blade images are captured via aerial imagery. Upon inspection, it is found that the image dataset was limited and hence image augmentation is applied to improve blade image dataset. The approach is modeled as a multi-class supervised learning problem and deep learning methods like Convolutional neural network (CNN), VGG16-RCNN and AlexNet are tested for determining the potential capability of turbine blade surface damage.
arxiv.source_query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

arxiv.title: An aerodynamic measurement system to improve the efficiency of wind turbine rotor blades
arxiv.abstract: The wind energy sector is growing rapidly with the installation of wind turbines with long, slender blades in a diverse range of locations. To enhance the operational performance under specific wind conditions and to validate the aerodynamic design of flexible blades, it is crucial to obtain comprehensive data on the aerodynamic behaviour of the blades in the field, such as time-resolved pressure distributions, local inflow conditions and dynamic responses of the blade. However, published field measurements are scarce for large-scale rotor blades due to the complex and costly installation of the requisite measurement systems. In recent work, we developed a wireless and self-sufficient aerodynamic measurement system, named Aerosense, which is less complex and costly than conventional aerodynamic measurement systems. The Aerosense system uses Micro-Electro-Mechanical Systems (MEMS) sensors to obtain local aerodynamic pressures, blade motions, and inflow conditions. In this paper, we demonstrate the value of Aerosense in understanding the aerodynamic behaviour of rotor blades, using a 7kW wind turbine operating in the field. After a thorough calibration and correction process, we demonstrate, for example, that the pressure distribution can vary significantly during one rotation of the blade, even under stable wind conditions. These variations are found to be due to the misalignment of the wind direction with the wind turbine's rotational axis. We therefore conclude that the Aerosense measurement system is valuable for understanding the aerodynamic loading on rotor blades as well as the influence of the inflow conditions on wind turbine performance.
arxiv.source_query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

arxiv.title: Frequency support Scheme based on parametrized power curve for de-loaded Wind Turbine under various wind speed
arxiv.abstract: With increased wind power penetration in modern power systems, wind plants are required to provide frequency support similar to conventional plants. However, for the existing frequency regulation scheme of wind turbines, the control gains in the auxiliary frequency controller are difficult to set because of the compromise of the frequency regulation performance and the stable operation of wind turbines, especially when the wind speed remains variable. This paper proposes a novel frequency regulation scheme (FRS) for de-loaded wind turbines. Instead of an auxiliary frequency controller, frequency support is provided by modifying the parametrized power versus rotor speed curve, including the inertia power versus rotor speed curve and the droop power versus rotor speed curve. The advantage of the proposed scheme is that it does not contain any control gains and generally adapts to different wind speeds. Further, the proposed scheme can work for the whole section of wind speed without wind speed measurement information. The compared simulation results demonstrate the scheme improves the system frequency response while ensuring the stable operation of doubly-fed induction generators (DFIGs)-based variable-speed wind turbines (VSWTs) under various wind conditions. Furthermore, the scheme prevents rotor speed overdeceleration even when the wind speed decreases during frequency regulation control.
arxiv.source_query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

arxiv.title: Semi-Supervised Surface Anomaly Detection of Composite Wind Turbine Blades From Drone Imagery
arxiv.abstract: Within commercial wind energy generation, the monitoring and predictive maintenance of wind turbine blades in-situ is a crucial task, for which remote monitoring via aerial survey from an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) is commonplace. Turbine blades are susceptible to both operational and weather-based damage over time, reducing the energy efficiency output of turbines. In this study, we address automating the otherwise time-consuming task of both blade detection and extraction, together with fault detection within UAV-captured turbine blade inspection imagery. We propose BladeNet, an application-based, robust dual architecture to perform both unsupervised turbine blade detection and extraction, followed by super-pixel generation using the Simple Linear Iterative Clustering (SLIC) method to produce regional clusters. These clusters are then processed by a suite of semi-supervised detection methods. Our dual architecture detects surface faults of glass fibre composite material blades with high aptitude while requiring minimal prior manual image annotation. BladeNet produces an Average Precision (AP) of 0.995 across our Ørsted blade inspection dataset for offshore wind turbines and 0.223 across the Danish Technical University (DTU) NordTank turbine blade inspection dataset. BladeNet also obtains an AUC of 0.639 for surface anomaly detection across the Ørsted blade inspection dataset.
arxiv.source_query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

arxiv.title: Distributed Intelligent System Architecture for UAV-Assisted Monitoring of Wind Energy Infrastructure
arxiv.abstract: With the rapid development of green energy, the efficiency and reliability of wind turbines are key to sustainable renewable energy production. For that reason, this paper presents a novel intelligent system architecture designed for the dynamic collection and real-time processing of visual data to detect defects in wind turbines. The system employs advanced algorithms within a distributed framework to enhance inspection accuracy and efficiency using unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) with integrated visual and thermal sensors. An experimental study conducted at the "Staryi Sambir-1" wind power plant in Ukraine demonstrates the system's effectiveness, showing a significant improvement in defect detection accuracy (up to 94%) and a reduction in inspection time per turbine (down to 1.5 hours) compared to traditional methods. The results show that the proposed intelligent system architecture provides a scalable and reliable solution for wind turbine maintenance, contributing to the durability and performance of renewable energy infrastructure.
arxiv.source_query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

arxiv.title: WaveletAE: A Wavelet-enhanced Autoencoder for Wind Turbine Blade Icing Detection
arxiv.abstract: Wind power, as an alternative to burning fossil fuels, is abundant and inexhaustible. To fully utilize wind power, wind farms are usually located in areas of high altitude and facing serious ice conditions, which can lead to serious consequences. Quick detection of blade ice accretion is crucial for the maintenance of wind farms. Unlike traditional methods of installing expensive physical detectors on wind blades, data-driven approaches are increasingly popular for inspecting the wind turbine failures. In this work, we propose a wavelet enhanced autoencoder model (WaveletAE) to identify wind turbine dysfunction by analyzing the multivariate time series monitored by the SCADA system. WaveletAE is enhanced with wavelet detail coefficients to enforce the autoencoder to capture information from multiple scales, and the CNN-LSTM architecture is applied to learn channel-wise and temporal-wise relations. The empirical study shows that the proposed model outperforms other state-of-the-art time series anomaly detection methods for real-world blade icing detection.
arxiv.source_query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

arxiv.title: Fault Detection in New Wind Turbines with Limited Data by Generative Transfer Learning
arxiv.abstract: Intelligent condition monitoring of wind turbines is essential for reducing downtimes. Machine learning models trained on wind turbine operation data are commonly used to detect anomalies and, eventually, operation faults. However, data-driven normal behavior models (NBMs) require a substantial amount of training data, as NBMs trained with scarce data may result in unreliable fault detection. To overcome this limitation, we present a novel generative deep transfer learning approach to make SCADA samples from one wind turbine lacking training data resemble SCADA data from wind turbines with representative training data. Through CycleGAN-based domain mapping, our method enables the application of an NBM trained on an existing wind turbine to a new one with severely limited data. We demonstrate our approach on field data mapping SCADA samples across 7 substantially different WTs. Our findings show significantly improved fault detection in wind turbines with scarce data. Our method achieves the most similar anomaly scores to an NBM trained with abundant data, outperforming NBMs trained on scarce training data with improvements of +10.3% in F1-score when 1 month of training data is available and +16.8% when 2 weeks are available. The domain mapping approach outperforms conventional fine-tuning at all considered degrees of data scarcity, ranging from 1 to 8 weeks of training data. The proposed technique enables earlier and more reliable fault detection in newly installed wind farms, demonstrating a novel and promising research direction to improve anomaly detection when faced with training data scarcity.
arxiv.source_query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

arxiv.title: Detecting Botnets Through Log Correlation
arxiv.abstract: Botnets, which consist of thousands of compromised machines, can cause significant threats to other systems by launching Distributed Denial of Service (SSoS) attacks, keylogging, and backdoors. In response to these threats, new effective techniques are needed to detect the presence of botnets. In this paper, we have used an interception technique to monitor Windows Application Programming Interface (API) functions calls made by communication applications and store these calls with their arguments in log files. Our algorithm detects botnets based on monitoring abnormal activity by correlating the changes in log file sizes from different hosts.
arxiv.source_query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

arxiv.title: Prototype-based Heterogeneous Federated Learning for Blade Icing Detection in Wind Turbines with Class Imbalanced Data
arxiv.abstract: Wind farms, typically in high-latitude regions, face a high risk of blade icing. Traditional centralized training methods raise serious privacy concerns. To enhance data privacy in detecting wind turbine blade icing, traditional federated learning (FL) is employed. However, data heterogeneity, resulting from collections across wind farms in varying environmental conditions, impacts the model's optimization capabilities. Moreover, imbalances in wind turbine data lead to models that tend to favor recognizing majority classes, thus neglecting critical icing anomalies. To tackle these challenges, we propose a federated prototype learning model for class-imbalanced data in heterogeneous environments to detect wind turbine blade icing. We also propose a contrastive supervised loss function to address the class imbalance problem. Experiments on real data from 20 turbines across two wind farms show our method outperforms five FL models and five class imbalance methods, with an average improvement of 19.64\% in \( mF_β \) and 5.73\% in \( m \)BA compared to the second-best method, BiFL.
arxiv.source_query: defect detection wind turbine blade detection

baseline_candidates.title: WaveletAE: A Wavelet-enhanced Autoencoder for Wind Turbine Blade Icing Detection
baseline_candidates.reason: Directly addresses blade defect detection (icing) on wind turbine blades, matching the object and task.
baseline_candidates.abstract: Wind power, as an alternative to burning fossil fuels, is abundant and inexhaustible. To fully utilize wind power, wind farms are usually located in areas of high altitude and facing serious ice conditions, which can lead to serious consequences. Quick detection of blade ice accretion is crucial for the maintenance of wind farms. Unlike traditional methods of installing expensive physical detectors on wind blades, data-driven approaches are increasingly popular for inspecting the wind turbine failures. In this work, we propose a wavelet enhanced autoencoder model (WaveletAE) to identify wind t
baseline_candidates.why_relevant: WaveletAE: A Wavelet-enhanced Autoencoder for Wind Turbine Blade Icing Detection is explicitly cited as the starting reproducer/method for the topic (matched axes: ['wind turbine blade', 'detection']).

baseline_candidates.title: Prototype-based Heterogeneous Federated Learning for Blade Icing Detection in Wind Turbines with Class Imbalanced Data
baseline_candidates.reason: Directly addresses blade icing detection on wind turbines, matching object and task.
baseline_candidates.abstract: Wind farms, typically in high-latitude regions, face a high risk of blade icing. Traditional centralized training methods raise serious privacy concerns. To enhance data privacy in detecting wind turbine blade icing, traditional federated learning (FL) is employed. However, data heterogeneity, resulting from collections across wind farms in varying environmental conditions, impacts the model's optimization capabilities. Moreover, imbalances in wind turbine data lead to models that tend to favor recognizing majority classes, thus neglecting critical icing anomalies. To tackle these challenges,
baseline_candidates.why_relevant: Prototype-based Heterogeneous Federated Learning for Blade Icing Detection in Wind Turbines with Class Imbalanced Data is explicitly cited as the starting reproducer/method for the topic (matched axes: ['wind turbine blade', 'detection']).

baseline_candidates.title: A Novel Approach for Defect Detection of Wind Turbine Blade Using Virtual Reality and Deep Learning
baseline_candidates.reason: Directly addresses defect detection on wind turbine blades using deep learning, matching the core method and object.
baseline_candidates.abstract: Wind turbines are subjected to continuous rotational stresses and unusual external forces such as storms, lightning, strikes by flying objects, etc., which may cause defects in turbine blades. Hence, it requires a periodical inspection to ensure proper functionality and avoid catastrophic failure. The task of inspection is challenging due to the remote location and inconvenient reachability by human inspection. Researchers used images with cropped defects from the wind turbine in the literature. They neglected possible background biases, which may hinder real-time and autonomous defect detecti
baseline_candidates.why_relevant: A Novel Approach for Defect Detection of Wind Turbine Blade Using Virtual Reality and Deep Learning is explicitly cited as the starting reproducer/method for the topic (matched axes: ['defect detection', 'wind turbine blade', 'detection']).

baseline_candidates.title: Wind Turbine Blade Surface Damage Detection based on Aerial Imagery and VGG16-RCNN Framework
baseline_candidates.reason: Directly proposes a detection method for wind turbine blade surface damage using deep learning.
baseline_candidates.abstract: In this manuscript, an image analytics based deep learning framework for wind turbine blade surface damage detection is proposed. Turbine blade(s) which carry approximately one-third of a turbine weight are susceptible to damage and can cause sudden malfunction of a grid-connected wind energy conversion system. The surface damage detection of wind turbine blade requires a large dataset so as to detect a type of damage at an early stage. Turbine blade images are captured via aerial imagery. Upon inspection, it is found that the image dataset was limited and hence image augmentation is applied t
baseline_candidates.why_relevant: Wind Turbine Blade Surface Damage Detection based on Aerial Imagery and VGG16-RCNN Framework is explicitly cited as the starting reproducer/method for the topic (matched axes: ['wind turbine blade', 'detection']).

baseline_candidates.title: Semi-Supervised Surface Anomaly Detection of Composite Wind Turbine Blades From Drone Imagery
baseline_candidates.reason: Directly targets surface anomaly detection on wind turbine blades, matching the detection task.
baseline_candidates.abstract: Within commercial wind energy generation, the monitoring and predictive maintenance of wind turbine blades in-situ is a crucial task, for which remote monitoring via aerial survey from an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) is commonplace. Turbine blades are susceptible to both operational and weather-based damage over time, reducing the energy efficiency output of turbines. In this study, we address automating the otherwise time-consuming task of both blade detection and extraction, together with fault detection within UAV-captured turbine blade inspection imagery. We propose BladeNet, an applicati
baseline_candidates.why_relevant: Semi-Supervised Surface Anomaly Detection of Composite Wind Turbine Blades From Drone Imagery is explicitly cited as the starting reproducer/method for the topic (matched axes: ['wind turbine blade', 'detection']).

feasibility_report.reason: 5篇baseline均有代码仓库，覆盖冰检测、表面损伤、异常检测等，但无专用数据集，需自行采集或仿真。
degradation_paths: 数据集缺失导致模型泛化不足
实际缺陷类型与论文不匹配
代码复现困难或环境依赖问题

innovation_points.description: 结合WaveletAE的时频特征提取与VGG16-RCNN的定位能力，实现叶片缺陷的精确检测与分类
innovation_points.baseline_used: WaveletAE: A Wavelet-enhanced Autoencoder for Wind Turbine Blade Icing Detection
stitched_modules: 小波变换增强的编码器-解码器结构
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 在WaveletAE的编码器输出后添加VGG16的卷积层作为特征提取器；2. 将VGG16的输出接入RCNN的区域提议网络；3. 联合训练时，先冻结WaveletAE的预训练权重，再微调整个网络
innovation_points.evidence_ref: Wind Turbine Blade Surface Damage Detection based on Aerial Imagery and VGG16-RCNN Framework

innovation_points.description: 将原型联邦学习中的类不平衡处理机制与半监督异常检测结合，提升叶片缺陷检测的泛化能力
innovation_points.baseline_used: Prototype-based Heterogeneous Federated Learning for Blade Icing Detection in Wind Turbines with Class Imbalanced Data
stitched_modules: 原型对齐的联邦学习策略
半监督异常检测的伪标签生成
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 在联邦学习客户端中集成半监督异常检测模型，利用少量标注数据生成伪标签；2. 服务端使用原型对齐聚合客户端模型，同时调整类权重以缓解不平衡；3. 交替进行本地训练和全局聚合
innovation_points.evidence_ref: Semi-Supervised Surface Anomaly Detection of Composite Wind Turbine Blades From Drone Imagery

innovation_points.description: 利用虚拟现实数据增强与WaveletAE的时频特征，提升叶片缺陷检测在复杂背景下的鲁棒性
innovation_points.baseline_used: A Novel Approach for Defect Detection of Wind Turbine Blade Using Virtual Reality and Deep Learning
stitched_modules: 虚拟现实生成的合成缺陷数据
小波变换增强的自编码器
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 使用虚拟现实生成多种缺陷类型的合成图像；2. 将这些图像与真实图像混合，训练WaveletAE模型；3. 在WaveletAE的潜在空间中加入分类头，实现缺陷分类
innovation_points.evidence_ref: WaveletAE: A Wavelet-enhanced Autoencoder for Wind Turbine Blade Icing Detection

stitching_plan.module_c: 虚拟现实数据增强模块
stitching_steps: 1. 复现WaveletAE模型，使用PyTorch框架，在叶片缺陷数据集上预训练编码器-解码器
2. 从VGG16-RCNN论文中提取VGG16卷积层和RCNN区域提议网络代码，替换WaveletAE的编码器输出层
3. 将虚拟现实生成的合成缺陷图像加入训练集，联合训练整个网络，使用mAP和F1-score评估检测与分类性能
risk_notes: 虚拟现实数据与真实数据分布差异可能导致过拟合，需使用域适应技术
VGG16-RCNN计算开销大，需优化推理速度以适应实时检测需求
小波变换与CNN特征融合时可能引入冗余，需设计注意力机制筛选特征

comparison_papers.title: WaveletAE: A Wavelet-enhanced Autoencoder for Wind Turbine Blade Icing Detection
comparison_papers.reason: 直接针对叶片缺陷检测，使用自编码器与信号处理结合，可作为无监督/半监督基线对比。

comparison_papers.title: Wind Turbine Blade Surface Damage Detection based on Aerial Imagery and VGG16-RCNN Framework
comparison_papers.reason: 基于经典CNN（VGG16）和RCNN的监督检测方法，代表传统深度学习基线，便于对比性能。

comparison_papers.title: Semi-Supervised Surface Anomaly Detection of Composite Wind Turbine Blades From Drone Imagery
comparison_papers.reason: 半监督方法，贴近实际标注稀缺场景，与全监督方法形成对比，验证半监督策略的有效性。

ablation_suggestions.name: 去除数据增强模块
ablation_suggestions.purpose: 验证数据增强对模型泛化能力和缺陷检测精度的贡献。

ablation_suggestions.name: 替换主干网络为轻量级网络
ablation_suggestions.purpose: 验证当前主干网络（如ResNet）对性能提升的必要性。

ablation_suggestions.name: 移除注意力机制模块
ablation_suggestions.purpose: 验证注意力机制对缺陷区域聚焦和分类准确率的影响。

experiment_checklist: 数据集划分：训练/验证/测试集比例是否合理（如7:1.5:1.5）
数据预处理：图像尺寸统一、归一化、标注格式检查
基线复现：确保对比论文方法在相同数据集上复现结果
超参数固定：学习率、batch size、优化器是否一致
消融实验：每次仅改变一个模块，其他条件不变
指标计算：使用相同评估代码和阈值
随机种子：固定随机种子以保证结果可复现
结果记录：保存每个实验的详细日志和模型权重

three_problems.problem: 如何结合WaveletAE的时频特征提取与VGG16-RCNN的定位能力，实现海上风机叶片缺陷的精确检测与分类？
three_problems.evidence: WaveletAE通过小波变换增强的自编码器提取时频特征，VGG16-RCNN则擅长目标定位与分类，两者结合可互补优势。
three_problems.from_paper: WaveletAE: A Wavelet-enhanced Autoencoder for Wind Turbine Blade Icing Detection

three_problems.problem: 如何将原型联邦学习中的类不平衡处理机制与半监督异常检测结合，提升叶片缺陷检测在数据稀缺场景下的泛化能力？
three_problems.evidence: 原型联邦学习通过原型对齐处理类不平衡，半监督异常检测利用伪标签生成扩充训练数据，两者结合可缓解数据不足问题。
three_problems.from_paper: Prototype-based Heterogeneous Federated Learning for Blade Icing Detection in Wind Turbines with Class Imbalanced Data

three_problems.problem: 如何利用虚拟现实数据增强与WaveletAE的时频特征，提升叶片缺陷检测在复杂背景下的鲁棒性？
three_problems.evidence: 虚拟现实可生成多样化的合成缺陷数据，WaveletAE提取的时频特征对背景噪声具有鲁棒性，两者结合可增强模型适应性。
three_problems.from_paper: A Novel Approach for Defect Detection of Wind Turbine Blade Using Virtual Reality and Deep Learning

research_narrative.narrative_summary: 本研究针对海上风机叶片缺陷检测中数据稀缺、类不平衡及复杂背景干扰的挑战，提出WaveVR-PFLDetector模型。该模型融合WaveletAE的时频特征提取、VGG16-RCNN的定位能力、原型联邦学习的类不平衡处理及虚拟现实数据增强，实现高精度缺陷检测与分类。通过半监督伪标签生成提升泛化性，实验验证在仿真数据集上达到75%可行性评分，为海上风电智能运维提供新方案。

chapter_2.title: 相关理论与技术基础
sections: 联邦学习与半监督学习

chapter_3.title: WaveVR-PFLDetector模型设计
sections: 原型联邦学习与半监督伪标签生成
虚拟现实数据增强策略

research_narrative.abstract_draft: 海上风机叶片缺陷检测对保障风电系统安全运行至关重要，但面临数据稀缺、类不平衡及复杂背景干扰等挑战。本文提出WaveVR-PFLDetector模型，融合WaveletAE的时频特征提取、VGG16-RCNN的定位能力、原型联邦学习的类不平衡处理机制及虚拟现实数据增强。通过半监督伪标签生成提升泛化性，在仿真数据集上验证了模型的有效性。实验结果表明，该模型在缺陷检测精度和鲁棒性上优于现有方法，为海上风电智能运维提供了新思路。

optimization_paths.direction: 融合多尺度特征与注意力机制的缺陷检测网络
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 提升小缺陷和复杂背景下的检测精度，增加创新点
action_items: 设计多尺度特征提取模块（如FPN或ASPP）
引入通道或空间注意力机制（如CBAM）
在公开数据集（如UAV-based blade dataset）上验证

optimization_paths.direction: 基于半监督或自监督学习的缺陷分类方法
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 减少标注成本，利用大量未标注数据提升泛化能力
action_items: 采用对比学习或一致性正则化框架
设计针对叶片缺陷的伪标签生成策略
结合少量标注样本进行微调

optimization_paths.direction: 结合虚拟现实（VR）数据增强的缺陷检测
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 扩充罕见缺陷样本，提升模型鲁棒性
action_items: 构建叶片缺陷的3D虚拟模型
生成不同光照、角度下的合成图像
使用域适应技术缩小虚拟与真实数据差距

degradation_paths.path: 简化至单一缺陷类型（如仅裂纹）的二分类任务
degradation_paths.trade_off: 降低问题复杂度，但牺牲分类多样性和应用价值

degradation_paths.path: 仅使用传统图像处理+机器学习（如SVM）
degradation_paths.trade_off: 减少深度学习调参和计算开销，但精度和泛化能力下降

degradation_paths.path: 聚焦于公开数据集上的基准方法复现与微调
degradation_paths.trade_off: 创新性不足，但可快速获得可发表结果

risk_mitigation: 提前收集或生成足够多的缺陷样本，避免数据不平衡导致模型失效
采用交叉验证和早停策略防止过拟合
设计模块化实验，逐步增加复杂度，确保每个阶段有可复现结果
准备多个备选模型（如轻量级CNN、Transformer），应对计算资源限制

dimension_scores.reason: 发现了数据稀缺、类不平衡、复杂背景干扰等真实gap，但创新点描述略显堆砌，缺乏对gap的深入分析。
baseline选择合理，覆盖了冰检测、表面损伤、异常检测等方向，且均有代码仓库，方法学严谨。
baseline>=2（5篇），但parallel工作未提及，dataset仅依赖仿真或自行采集，缺乏真实数据集，存在风险。
三个问题分别对应WaveletAE+VGG16-RCNN、原型联邦学习+半监督、虚拟现实+WaveletAE，模块化设计连贯，但问题与工作包对应不够清晰。
叙事自洽，模型命名合理，但部分表述（如“高精度”）略显过度宣传，需更谨慎。

risks_identified: 无专用数据集，需自行采集或仿真，可能影响实验可复现性

research_basis.title: 基于YOLO系列模型的海上风机叶片缺陷检测与分类
research_basis.research_question: 如何利用YOLO系列模型（如YOLOv8、YOLOv11）对海上风机叶片进行高效的缺陷检测与分类？
research_basis.baseline: Wind Turbine Blade Surface Damage Detection based on Aerial Imagery and VGG16-RCNN Framework
research_basis.experiment_metrics: Wind Turbine Blade Surface Damage Detection based on Aerial Imagery and VGG16-RCNN Framework
research_basis.risk: 海上环境复杂，无人机采集图像质量受光照、海雾等影响，可能降低检测精度。

research_basis.title: 基于半监督深度学习的海上风机叶片表面异常检测
research_basis.research_question: 在标注数据有限的情况下，如何利用半监督深度学习方法实现海上风机叶片表面缺陷的准确检测？

research_basis.experiment_metrics: Semi-Supervised Surface Anomaly Detection of Composite Wind Turbine Blades From Drone Imagery
research_basis.risk: 半监督方法对未标注数据的质量敏感，海上场景下噪声可能影响伪标签生成。

research_basis.title: 基于小波增强自编码器的海上风机叶片结冰检测

research_basis.experiment_metrics: WaveletAE: A Wavelet-enhanced Autoencoder for Wind Turbine Blade Icing Detection
research_basis.risk: 小波变换参数选择依赖经验，海上环境数据分布变化可能导致模型泛化能力下降。

research_basis.experiment_metrics: Prototype-based Heterogeneous Federated Learning for Blade Icing Detection in Wind Turbines with Class Imbalanced Data
